title: RPC vs REST, and contracts that last
intro: How to choose an API style and design a contract clients can depend on.

steps.title: Two styles: actions vs resources
steps.body:
- **RPC** — call a remote *action* (`createUser`, `sendPayment`). Feels like local code; tight coupling. **gRPC** (HTTP/2 + protobuf) is the fast, binary, strongly-typed modern form.
- **REST** — manipulate *resources* (`/users/42`) through a **uniform interface** of HTTP verbs. Stateless, cacheable, loosely coupled.

> [!key] REST wins for public, evolvable, cacheable web APIs. RPC/gRPC wins for fast, strongly-typed internal service-to-service calls.

steps.title: Pick the style by context
steps.body: There's no universal winner — match the style to the job:

points.label: REST
points.note: Public web APIs. Resource-centric, stateless, cacheable, loosely coupled, human-readable JSON. Evolves gracefully.

points.label: gRPC
points.note: Internal service-to-service. Action-centric, HTTP/2 + protobuf, binary + fast, strongly typed, streaming. Tighter coupling.

points.label: GraphQL
points.note: Client-shaped queries — exactly the fields you need in one round-trip. Great for varied frontends; caching + complexity cost.

steps.title: Idempotency makes retries safe
steps.body:
**Safety** = no side effects; **idempotency** = repeating the call lands the same state.

| Verb | Op | Safe | Idempotent |
|---|---|---|---|
| GET | Read | ✅ | ✅ |
| POST | Create | ❌ | ❌ |
| PUT | Replace | ❌ | ✅ |
| DELETE | Remove | ❌ | ✅ |

> [!tip] A dropped response makes the client retry. If the call is idempotent (PUT/DELETE), replaying it can't double-charge. For non-idempotent POST (payments), pass an **idempotency key** so the server dedupes. This is the link back to time-redundancy retries.

steps.title: Design the contract to evolve
steps.body:
An API is a promise — once clients depend on it, adding is easy, changing is hard.

- **Model resources, not actions**: `POST /users/42/orders`, not `/createOrderForUser`.
- **Pagination** — never return an unbounded list.
- **Versioning** — `/v2/...` or headers, so you can evolve without breaking clients.
- **Predictable errors** — consistent status codes + a stable error body.

recap:
**Default to REST for public APIs; reach for gRPC on hot internal paths.**

- RPC = actions / tight coupling; REST = resources / uniform verbs / loose coupling.
- Make writes **idempotent** (or add an idempotency key) so retries are safe.
- Bake in **pagination, versioning, and stable errors** before the first client ships.
- Endpoints come from the boundary-crossing messages in your sequence diagrams.
